18 June

Happy Birthday,
Kajal

It's been just over a year since I saw a photograph of you and couldn't stop myself from reaching out. Here is everything that's happened since.

Our story

Where it began

17 June 2025

The photograph

I saw this picture and knew I had to talk to you. Then, mid-conversation, you got pulled into a call with your parents — flustered, trying to juggle both at once. That was it for me.

July 2025

Face to face

A few weeks of talking later, we finally met in person. I don't remember most of what we said that day — only that I didn't want it to end.

Somewhere in those early weeks — dressed up, already impossible to look away from.

15 September 2025

An ordinary day, made better

Just a regular evening after a good day at your office. Nothing special was supposed to happen — except with you, somehow, it always did.

Bedhead, mid-laugh, completely unposed. Still my favourite version of you.

Half-smiling at something off-screen. I still don't know what was so funny.

The engagement

We said yes

Then came this day — the one where it stopped being "we'll see" and started being certain.

One of our trips in between. Just us, no occasion required.

25 April 2026

The wedding

Amid all that noise and chaos, this is the photo that survived — two people trying to find each other in the middle of their own wedding.

May 2026

First trip, as husband and wife

Andaman, a few days in. Married, official, and still getting used to saying "my wife."

June 2026 — today

Married life

And here you are now. Married life clearly suits you. Happy birthday, Kajal.

Bonus

A Few You'll Pretend Not to Remember

Some moments don't need a date. They just need to be teased about, forever.

The hangry face.
Feed her before it's too late.

The "we are not
speaking right now" look.

Mid-sentence, mid-something,
somewhere with a view.

Face mask, peace sign,
zero regrets.

One year since that photograph. One wedding, one trip, one whole life now shared. Here's to every ordinary day still ahead of us — may they keep turning into something worth remembering.

Happy birthday, my love.

— Lokesh

18 June 2026
